title: podman build fails on Windows with volume binding
body:
### Issue Description

Ran `podman build . -v %CD%:/dir1 -t tmp`

and got 

`Error: validating volumes: invalid host path, must be an absolute path "C"`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create Containerfile with FROM scratch
2. Run `podman build . -v %CD%:/dir1 -t tmp`


### Describe the results you received

`Error: validating volumes: invalid host path, must be an absolute path "C"`

### Describe the results you expected

Build should run

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Same problem here with podman *build*. I also tried specyfying the path with forward slash only. Or trying to prefix with /mnt/c/
Didn't work.
Error: validating volumes: faccessat /Users/: no such file or directory

Please do note that it's working fine with podman *run*
